mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing starts rate above 1.3 million signals a strengthening demand for housing that compresses mortgage rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 100 signals a weakening supply chain that compresses inventory turnover, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in the employment rate below 155 million signals a weakening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real GDP growth rate above 2.5% signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that compresses interest rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: strengthening demand for goods and services
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 3-month decrease in the inflation rate below 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and reduces inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tightening monetary cycle with low inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
